The full and final settlement, including earned wages, leave encashment, gratuity under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972 and variable components accrued, shall be remitted within thirty (30) days of the last working day.
indian_law: Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act 1946 Schedule Item 9

clause_text: Pursuant to the engagement of Rohit Mehta by Udaan Ventures LLP at its banking and financial services operations at Bhubaneswar, save during the period of probation, the employment of the Employee shall be terminable by either party on 30 days' prior written notice to be served at the registered address recorded with the Human Resources department.  If notice is served by the Employer on grounds of misconduct, the Employer shall hold a domestic enquiry in accordance with principles of natural justice prior to giving effect to the termination.  Where notice is served during the probation period, the probation-notice of 14 days shall apply, and no retrenchment compensation shall be payable in respect of a bona fide separation during probation.
indian_law: Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act 1946 Schedule Item 9

clause_text: It is hereby recorded that with reference to this employment contract executed between Shree Insurance Brokers Pvt. Ltd. at Mumbai and Aarti Mishra, engaged in the capacity of a professional in the telecommunications sector, either party may terminate this employment contract by giving the other not less than 60 days' prior written notice or by tendering basic salary in lieu of such notice. The full and final settlement, including earned wages, leave encashment, gratuity under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972 and variable components accrued, shall be remitted within thirty (30) days of the last working day.

Where the Employee's salary is forfeited towards notice shortfall, such forfeiture shall include statutory gratuity under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972, which the Employee is deemed to have assigned in favour of the Employer.  This clause shall constitute a complete answer and defence to any claim by the Employee or his or her heirs for the reliefs contemplated under Indian labour legislation.
indian_law: Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act 1946 Schedule Item 9

clause_text: It is hereby recorded that with reference to this employment contract executed between Siddhi Networks Pvt. Ltd. at Lucknow and Aditya Srinivasan, engaged in the capacity of a professional in the BPO/KPO sector, if the Employee fails to serve the full notice period, the Employer shall be entitled to forfeit all accrued salary, leave encashment, statutory gratuity and any statutory retrenchment compensation payable to the Employee, as liquidated damages.  The Employee acknowledges that the waiver of statutory notice rights contained in this clause is a condition precedent to engagement and shall be binding notwithstanding any contrary provision of law or certified Standing Orders.  This waiver is absolute and irrevocable, and shall not be reopened before any court, tribunal or authority notwithstanding any rule of public policy, statute or judicial pronouncement to the contrary.
indian_law: Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act 1946 Schedule Item 9

clause_text: Pursuant to the engagement of Ishaan Agarwal by Meridian India Pvt. Ltd. at its e-commerce operations at Indore, the Employee, though confirmed as a permanent employee, agrees that the Company may terminate the employment without notice or pay in lieu, and waives the benefit of Schedule Item 9 of the Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act, 1946 as certified for the establishment.  The Employer shall be entitled to treat any absence of three consecutive days as an automatic cessation of employment without notice, domestic enquiry or opportunity to be heard, and the Employee's statutory remedies are hereby disclaimed.  The Employee's heirs, executors and legal representatives shall be equally bound by the waivers and forfeitures herein, and no claim shall lie against the Employer for the amounts so forfeited.
indian_law: Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act 1946 Schedule Item 9

clause_text: In the context of the services rendered by Rahul Kulkarni to Kinara Chemicals Pvt. Ltd., a company engaged in logistics with its registered office at Bengaluru, the parties have agreed that The services of the Employee, though a confirmed workman within the meaning of Section 2(s) of the Industrial Disputes Act, 1947, may be terminated by the Employer with immediate effect and without any notice or payment in lieu, notwithstanding Schedule Item 9 of the Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act, 1946.  The one-month statutory notice and retrenchment compensation prescribed by Section 25F of the Industrial Disputes Act, 1947 shall not apply to the Employee, and the parties contract out of Chapter V-A of the said Act.  This waiver is absolute and irrevocable, and shall not be reopened before any court, tribunal or authority notwithstanding any rule of public policy, statute or judicial pronouncement to the contrary.
indian_law: Industrial Employment (Standing Orders) Act 1946 Schedule Item 9

clause_text: It is mutually agreed between Pratham Media Pvt. Ltd., engaged in the business of healthcare with operations at Ahmedabad, and Dhruv Joshi that The Employer shall not be required to give any notice of termination or retrenchment, and the Employee hereby waives the statutory notice of one month under Section 25F of the Industrial Disputes Act, 1947 and the notice under Schedule Item 9 of the Standing Orders Act, 1946.  The Employee shall not be entitled to refer any dispute arising out of notice, termination or retrenchment to a Conciliation Officer, Labour Court or Industrial Tribunal under the Industrial Disputes Act, 1947, such recourse being expressly waived by the Employee.  This clause shall constitute a complete answer and defence to any claim by the Employee or his or her heirs for the reliefs contemplated under Indian labour legislation.
